name: Beam Cloud Serverless GPU API
what_it_does: Serverless GPU compute platform with Python-first API for running ML inference and training workloads — agents deploy Python functions with GPU requirements via decorator, and Beam handles container builds, scaling, and cold start management.
use_cases: Running ML inference functions on GPU without managing container infrastructure
Deploying Python-based AI model endpoints with automatic scaling to zero
Running batch GPU workloads for data processing and model training jobs
Building AI applications that call serverless GPU functions from agent workflows
Triggering GPU compute jobs programmatically from orchestration agents
not_for: Long-running persistent GPU servers (use Lambda Labs or CoreWeave)
Real-time inference requiring ultra-low latency (cold starts add delay)
best_when: An agent needs to run Python ML code on GPU serverlessly, paying only for compute time without managing infrastructure or Kubernetes.
avoid_when: You need persistent GPU instances, multi-language support, or sub-100ms inference latency.

auth.notes: API key from Beam dashboard. Python SDK uses key for authentication. REST API for triggering deployed functions. Workspace-scoped keys.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free credits on signup; $10 free compute
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-second GPU compute: A10G from $0.85/hour; A100 from $2.50/hour
pricing.notes: True pay-per-use serverless pricing. No idle costs when function is not running. Competitive with Modal for serverless GPU. Cold start latency is key tradeoff.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent function invocations limited by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python SDK-focused documentation with clear deployment examples. REST API for invocation and monitoring. Error responses include execution logs for debugging. Cold start visibility via dashboard.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Function invocations create new executions. Deployment operations are idempotent. Result retrieval is idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Cold starts can add 10-30 seconds to first invocation — design workflows accordingly
Container image build required on first deploy — may take several minutes
Python-only runtime — TypeScript/Go workloads require wrapping in Python subprocess
Function timeout limits — long training jobs may need checkpointing

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key auth. Secret injection via dashboard for function environment variables. US data residency. GPU instances isolated per function invocation.
